IMPORTANT: Ultra-realistic photography only. Real human subject. No anime, no cartoon, no illustration, no CGI, no 3D render, no doll-like face, no artificial beauty filter, no exaggerated features.

Create an ultra-realistic cozy Japanese bedroom scene inspired by modern Japanese drama aesthetics. The atmosphere is warm, peaceful, feminine, and naturally lived-in.
Scene & Environment: I am lying comfortably on a soft bed inside a beautiful Japanese-style bedroom. Warm golden sunlight streams through a large window, creating soft light rays and gentle shadows across the room. Outside the window, lush green trees are visible, adding a fresh and calming feeling.
Near me is a beautiful vintage radio with realistic retro details. The room contains several fluffy pillows, small cute decorative items, tiny flower arrangements, a compact wooden bookshelf filled with books, aesthetic trinkets, cozy fabric textures, and charming feminine room decorations. The interior feels like the bedroom of a young Japanese woman in a heartwarming slice-of-life drama, with natural realism and tasteful styling.
Small wildflowers and delicate decorative plants are placed around the room. Everything feels cozy, warm, elegant, and photorealistic without looking staged.
Pose & Expression: Lying naturally on the bed, relaxed and comfortable. Head slightly turned toward the camera. Soft gentle smile with calm eyes. Natural candid feeling as if enjoying a quiet afternoon at home.
Hair: Long straight black hair, smooth, silky, healthy, naturally spread across the pillow with realistic strands catching the sunlight.
Makeup: Premium Korean-Japanese Douyin inspired makeup, soft glowing skin, subtle blush, delicate eyelashes, natural glossy lips, refined eye makeup, youthful and elegant beauty, realistic skin texture, no heavy makeup, no plastic skin.
Outfit: Oversized white t-shirt with the word "Avelyrahn" printed on the front, accompanied by a simple elegant tree illustration. Comfortable casual homewear style, realistic fabric folds and textures.
Accessories: Minimalist silver necklace, delicate silver rings, elegant silver bracelet, subtle and refined accessories that complement the cozy aesthetic.
Photography: Ultra-realistic professional photography, natural window lighting, cinematic composition, shallow depth of field, realistic skin pores, authentic fabric textures, soft highlights, warm color grading, premium lifestyle photography, Japanese drama atmosphere, cozy afternoon mood, high detail, 8K quality, masterpiece, photorealistic.

Her expression is dreamy and softly tired, eyes lifted toward the camera as if she just looked up from scrolling, lips gently closed in a relaxed pout.

Shot in a vertical 3:4 frame at slightly above eye level, medium close-up to three-quarter portrait. Warm dim tungsten room light mixed with cool phone-screen reflections, no flash, soft falloff across the couch and wall. Shallow depth of field, soft low-light grain, slight motion blur, natural imperfect sharpness. Background: plain beige-gray wall, minimal decor, late-night atmosphere. Soft glam makeup: subtle eyeliner, long lashes, smooth skin, glossy pink-nude lips. Realistic social-media night portrait aesthetic.

His pose should be relaxed, slightly leaning, with one hand on the chair’s backrest. His face should be turned about 45 degrees to the side, looking realistic. She is holding a clearly visible matcha drink. The background should be plain, simple, and modern.

Edit the photo while preserving the subject’s exact facial features and identity. Create a high-resolution vertical portrait composition (9:16), ultra-detailed, sharp focus throughout, no background blur, rendered with a premium 8K editorial finish.

Design the image as a sophisticated black-and-white fashion portrait collage arranged in a 2×3 grid, featuring six unique frames of the same young woman in a clean, minimalist indoor studio environment. The overall aesthetic should feel elegant, cinematic, intimate, and effortlessly stylish, inspired by timeless monochrome fashion editorials and luxury magazine photography.

Hair is long, reaching the waist, colored a cool ash-brown with subtle gray undertones. Styled in a Korean-inspired hush cut with soft face-framing layers, airy see-through bangs, and sleek straight lengths that gently curve outward at the ends. The texture appears silky, healthy, and glossy, with a few natural flyaway strands for realism.

Beauty styling remains refined and understated: luminous hydrated skin, naturally feathered brows, subtle brown eyeliner, soft mascara, muted nude lips with a velvety finish, and barely-there blush for a fresh editorial appearance.

Wardrobe consists of a fitted white rib-knit tank top paired with relaxed high-waisted vintage-wash denim jeans, visible in selected frames. Accessories include matte black nail art, delicate silver hoop earrings, multiple silver rings, and a slim silver wristwatch, contributing to a contemporary fashion-editorial mood.

Collage Frame Concepts:

• Frame 1 — Tight portrait crop, fingertips resting softly against the cheek, direct eye contact, confident yet gentle expression.

• Frame 2 — Casual seated pose on a sofa, body turned slightly to one side, gaze directed away from the camera in a contemplative moment.

• Frame 3 — Relaxed reclining position with one knee bent, leaning comfortably into an arm, creating a graceful editorial silhouette.

• Frame 4 — Arms lifted behind the head, posture open and self-assured, subtle lean backward conveying effortless confidence.

• Frame 5 — Emotional close portrait with a slight head tilt and closed eyes, emphasizing calmness and quiet introspection.

• Frame 6 — Front-facing seated composition with composed expression and subtle movement through the hair for a natural, candid feel.

Environment remains intentionally simple: a light-toned studio wall with a neutral sofa appearing selectively across certain frames. The background should support the subject without drawing attention away from her.

Lighting is soft and diffused, resembling natural window light in a professional studio. Gentle directional shadows create depth and dimensionality, while a faint rim light subtly separates the hair from the background. Avoid harsh flash or strong contrast.

Captured with the quality and detail associated with professional mirrorless cameras such as a Canon EOS R5 or Sony A7R IV. Use a combination of intimate close-ups and medium-length portraits, primarily at eye level with occasional slightly elevated angles. Emphasize balanced magazine-style compositions and natural visual flow across the collage.

Final processing should feature a rich monochrome conversion with smooth tonal transitions, lifted shadows, restrained contrast, delicate 35mm film grain, a soft matte finish, and exceptional detail retention in both skin texture and hair, echoing the look of classic high-fashion editorial photography.

8K UHD hyper-realistic commercial food photography, 3:4 aspect ratio. 6 scenes, each on its own solid or gradient background:

Scene 1, Döner slice explosion: Traditional Turkish döner (beef and lamb mix), paper-thin ribbons spiraling outward mid-air, white garlic sauce and red chili sauce splashing, fresh parsley leaves floating. Deep crimson red background.

Scene 2, Dürüm wrap floating: Premium dürüm cut in half and floating vertically, cross-section revealing döner meat, lettuce, tomatoes, onions layered inside, white garlic yogurt sauce drizzling elegantly, subtle spice particles drifting. Warm terracotta orange background.

Scene 3, Sauce pour drama: Mound of freshly sliced döner with crispy charred edges, thick creamy garlic yogurt sauce pouring from above frozen mid-flow, spicy red chili sauce drizzling alongside in thin crimson streams, sliced tomatoes and parsley below, heat vapor rising. Dark charcoal black background.

Scene 4, Deconstructed composition: Toasted lavash bread pieces, döner slices, tomato slices, lettuce leaves, and onion rings all suspended separately at varying heights, glossy sauce ribbons connecting elements artistically, ultra-fine spice dust in the air. Muted sage green background.

Scene 5, Rotating spit close-up: Extreme close-up of vertical döner tower on spit, large döner knife frozen mid-slice, fresh slice falling away, charred bits and seasoning particles in air, heat vapor rising from the fresh cut. Rich golden amber background.

Scene 6, Overhead plate explosion: Top-down view, all ingredients bursting upward in circular pattern, döner slices, french fries, grilled peppers and tomatoes, fresh parsley, sumac, lemon wedges, sauce droplets spraying, elements at varying heights with some rotating. Deep burgundy red background with vignette.

Global: controlled studio lighting emphasizing meat texture and char marks, shallow to medium depth of field, rich contrast, warm savory tones, natural shine, appetizing color grading. No text, logos, people, hands, cartoon style, or plastic-looking food.

Use the uploaded portrait as the identity reference and preserve the person's recognizable facial features, hairstyle, skin tone, expression, fashion sense, and overall presence. Create a premium full-body portrait of the same person alongside a large custom-designed plush companion that feels like their mascot alter ego. The plush should be inspired by the subject's mood, facial impression, styling, posture, and overall energy rather than being a generic animal or mascot. Automatically choose a creature concept that best matches the person's unique vibe, avoiding predictable or stereotype-based selections. The mascot must clearly be an oversized plush toy with soft fuzzy fabrics, rounded shapes, detailed stitching, premium textures, and a collectible designer-toy aesthetic. Its design, expression, silhouette, and proportions should subtly reflect the person's character and visual identity. Build a harmonious color palette using cues from the subject's hair, skin tone, clothing, and atmosphere so the person, mascot, and scene feel naturally connected. Show both the person and plush fully visible from head to toe, including shoes and all parts of the mascot, with balanced framing and comfortable spacing. Choose a natural interaction that suits the subject, such as standing beside, sitting with, leaning on, lightly hugging, or casually engaging with the plush companion. Keep the person's expression relaxed, warm, and authentic with a subtle smile or calm gaze, avoiding stiff poses or mannequin-like appearances. If the original image only shows part of the outfit, intelligently complete the full look in a believable and stylish way. Place the scene in a clean, aesthetically pleasing environment such as a minimalist studio, cozy lifestyle setting, or refined editorial backdrop that complements both the person and mascot without distractions. The final image should feel charming, cozy, stylish, emotionally engaging, visually cohesive, and suitable for a high-end character campaign or social-media editorial. Avoid cropped bodies, hidden shoes, incomplete mascot visibility, generic animal choices, real animals, horror elements, cheap toy aesthetics, awkward poses, cluttered backgrounds, distorted anatomy, extra limbs, text, logos, or watermarks.

"Create a single vertical anime development board for an original emotional short film titled "One More Player". Output ONE combined image with 2 sections: top section = character design sheet, bottom section = cinematic storyboard page.
STYLE: ultra high-quality nostalgic late 90s / early 2000s anime movie look, premium hand-drawn animation style, beautifully painted backgrounds, rich environmental detail, cinematic composition, soft but highly refined shading, natural character anatomy, expressive faces, subtle texture, realistic wet ground reflections, warm cloudy sunset lighting, atmospheric depth, polished old-anime film aesthetic, visually stunning frame quality, clean studio-level pre-production presentation. Make it feel like a premium old anime film, not modern glossy anime.
IMPORTANT: create fully original characters. Do not copy any existing anime, manga, game, or sports anime characters, faces, hairstyles, outfits, or famous designs.
SECTION A: CHARACTER DESIGN SHEET
Show 5 original characters clearly and consistently.
Main child:
A shy little boy around 8 years old with short messy dark hair, large warm eyes, slim build, oversized yellow T-shirt, blue shorts, and small sneakers. He feels nervous at first, then happy and accepted.
Show:
- front view
- side view
- back view
- 3/4 view
- expressions: nervous, scared, surprised, soft smile, happy
- poses: holding soccer ball, standing shyly, running with ball
Teen group:
Four teenage boys around 14 to 16, sporty and friendly, each with different hairstyles and casual soccer outfits. They should look slightly intimidating at first from the little boy’s perspective, but actually kind and welcoming. One teen is the warm leader.
Show for the teen group:
- simple front, side, back, and 3/4 views
- relaxed poses
- soccer poses
- smiling expressions
Keep them original, visually distinct, and cohesive.
Add small handwritten design notes and simple color swatches.
SECTION B: STORYBOARD PAGE
Create 8 cinematic storyboard panels in a clean grid. Use red panel borders, blue motion arrows, handwritten camera notes, timing notes, and short action notes. Keep all characters consistent.
Story beats:
1. Four teenagers playing soccer on a neighborhood ground after light rain, warm cloudy sunset, wet reflections on the field.
2. The soccer ball flies away toward the sitting area beside the field.
3. A shy little boy sitting alone notices the ball and picks it up.
4. The teenage boys walk toward him together. From the little boy’s view, they look a little scary.
5. Close-up of the little boy holding the ball with a nervous face.
6. The warm leader smiles kindly and gestures with his hand, inviting the boy to come play.
7. The little boy’s face changes from scared to relieved, then he smiles and runs toward them.
8. Final wide shot: the little boy happily plays soccer with the four teens under warm sunset light.
ENVIRONMENT:
Neighborhood soccer ground, wet dusty field, simple goalpost, sitting area beside the field, chain-link fence, trees, cloudy sunset sky, calm old-anime mood.
FINAL GOAL:
Make this a clean, highly polished, premium-quality anime pre-production board with beautiful cinematic presentation, emotional storytelling, strong readability, and a heartwarming ending. The image should look visually rich, refined, and high-end."

Use @image_1 as the authoritative director-approved storyboard blueprint for the sequence. Treat every storyboard panel as a consecutive shot within a single cinematic sequence. Follow panel order exactly and do not invent alternative coverage. Do not render the storyboard sheet itself. Preserve camera placement, framing, lens intent, shot scale, character staging, toy placement, chase geography, storm-drain danger logic, action escalation, continuity, and emotional clarity shown by the storyboard. The storyboard is the primary source of truth for visual storytelling. Recreate the filmed sequence implied by the panels rather than the physical storyboard artwork.

REFERENCE PRIORITY: @image_1 controls C1 cat identity, the stuffed toy mouse design, apartment warmth, city-street geography, chase direction, storm-drain danger, nine-lives ghost-silhouette logic, action escalation, emotional clarity, and panel purity.

TITLE: NINE LIVES. ONE MISSION.

FORMAT: 15-second cinematic 3D animated action-comedy short. One continuous fast rescue burst. No dialogue.

C1 CHARACTER: A tiny orange tabby kitten-like house cat with soft fluffy fur, round expressive eyes, white paws, white chest, striped orange coat, and an adorable but intensely serious face once the mission begins. Keep the same orange tabby identity throughout. C1 should feel like a real action hero trapped inside a tiny domestic body.

TOY CONTINUITY: The rescued object is a small grey stuffed toy mouse with a red string tail. It is clearly a plush toy, not a real mouse. It should feel worn, loved, and emotionally important. Throughout the chase it slides, tumbles, bounces, rolls, and skids from momentum only — it never moves on its own.

ENVIRONMENT: Start and end inside a warm apartment on a peaceful Saturday morning — soft golden sunlight, cozy cat bed, wood floor, plants, window light, domestic stillness. The middle sequence drops into a sunlit city street below — awnings, van roof, bicycle wheel, pedestrians, trash can, pigeons, wet pavement, storm drain, warm urban sunlight, dust, leaves, and cinematic motion.

EMOTIONAL GUIDANCE: Valence: cozy safety → sudden disaster → total mission focus → absurd heroic urgency → maximum danger at the drain → triumphant tiny victory → warm emotional softness. Arousal: calm opening, sharp alarm, immediate acceleration, escalating chase beats, slow-motion climax, victorious landing, gentle emotional release. The humor comes from the contrast between tiny domestic stakes and enormous action-movie seriousness. The audience should smile at how seriously the cat takes the mission, while still genuinely feeling the urgency and sweetness.

VISUAL STYLE: High-quality 3D animated film look. Pixar emotional clarity, The Incredibles energy, Mission Impossible seriousness, all applied to a tiny cat and a stuffed toy. Warm sunlight, expressive fur, readable facial performance, clean family-friendly action-comedy, playful cinematic action camera, soft detailed fur, clear geography, dynamic motion, strong readability. The key magical device is the nine lives: glowing ghost-cat silhouettes appear during the most extreme action moments. These ghost lives should feel magical, playful, heroic, and visually clear — never scary. They can peel away, trail behind, echo C1’s motion, and later snap back into C1.

AUDIO: No dialogue. Use cinematic family-action sound design only. Cozy apartment ambience at the start, toy slip and drop, tiny paw impacts, wind rush, awning bounce, van-roof slide scrape, bicycle near-miss, footsteps and pedestrian movement, trash can clatter, pigeon wing burst, city rush, brief tension build at the storm drain, slow-motion heartbeat and suspended air at the final leap, clean string-tail catch, tiny heroic landing thump, ghost-life shimmer, then warm quiet apartment ambience at the end. Music can be playful orchestral adventure: soft cozy opening, fast heroic build, strongest emotional swell at the storm-drain climax, triumphant tiny victory hit, then gentle sweet release.

PANEL BEATS:
P01: Peaceful morning; wide. C1 sleeps curled in a cozy bed beside the beloved grey stuffed toy mouse with red string tail. Warm apartment, golden sunlight, complete safety.
P02: Toy slips out window; medium close. The stuffed toy mouse slides over the edge of the open window and begins to fall. The red string tail is clear and readable.
P03: Disaster seen; close. C1 jolts awake and reaches in alarm as the falling toy drops away. This is the instant the calm world breaks.
P04: Mission lock-in; extreme close. C1’s eyes harden with total action-hero focus. The mission becomes absolutely serious.
P05: First leap / one life peels; epic wide. C1 launches out after the toy into the city void. The first glowing ghost-cat life peels off or flares behind the body, establishing the nine-lives visual device.
P06: Awning bounce; dynamic angle. C1 lands on a striped awning and rebounds forward. Keep the toy just ahead in the chase line. The moment is physical, funny, and heroic.
P07: Van rooftop slide; low track. C1 slides across the roof of a van, determined and low to the surface, still locked on the toy. Ghost-cat echoes can trail behind.
P08: Bicycle dodge; low angle. A bicycle wheel whips dangerously past as C1 flattens low and dodges cleanly. Keep the toy ahead and readable.
P09: Pedestrian weave; wide. C1 threads between moving legs and feet in a crowded street. The chase remains one clean forward line toward the toy.
P10: Trash can near miss; wide. A trash can spills and creates a chaotic obstacle. C1 narrowly avoids it and continues pursuit. Fast, readable, not violent.
P11: Pigeon burst; wide. C1 charges through a flock of pigeons. Wings and feathers explode upward around the cat and toy, selling cinematic scale.
P12: One life lost; medium. During a dangerous beat, one glowing ghost-cat life visibly peels away behind C1. It reads as magical sacrifice or heroic cost, playful rather than dark.
P13: Toy toward storm drain; low angle. The grey stuffed mouse skids toward the storm drain. The storm drain is now the clear main danger. The red string tail must read clearly.
P14: Final leap; epic wide. C1 commits to one impossible, emotionally charged leap toward the toy as it nears the drain. This is the biggest heroic beat in the film.
P15: Paw reaches string; extreme close. C1’s paw stretches toward the red string tail. Maximum tension, maximum clarity, slow-motion intensity.
P16: Last second catch; close. C1 catches the stuffed mouse by the red string tail at the very last possible second before it falls into the drain. This is the emotional peak.
P17: Hero landing; medium wide. C1 lands with the toy saved. Tiny superhero victory. Small shockwave, glowing ghost-cat silhouettes surrounding or snapping back into C1, triumphant but funny.
P18: Home. Mission complete; wide. Return to the warm apartment. C1 is dusty, messy, tired, proud, and sleeping peacefully while hugging the rescued stuffed toy mouse. End soft, sweet, warm, and emotionally satisfying.

KEY INSTRUCTIONS:

The whole short must feel like one clean escalating line: peace → accident → realization → pursuit → danger → last-second rescue → soft victory.
Keep the storm drain as the sharpest and clearest climax.
Protect emotional clarity above everything.
Keep the toy clearly reading as a plush toy at all times.
Keep C1 as the clear main character in every action beat.
Keep the same orange tabby identity throughout.
Keep the same grey stuffed mouse with red string tail throughout.
Do not make the toy seem alive.
Do not make the cat talk.
Do not make the action violent or scary.
Do not make the middle feel like random running shots; each action beat must push the same rescue forward.
Do not lose the storm drain as the main goal.
Do not overcomplicate the film with unrelated gags.
Do not show panel boxes, labels, numbers, arrows, captions, or storyboard graphics.
End on sweetness, pride, warmth, and mission-complete emotional satisfaction.
candidates.suggested_title: Use @image_1 as the authoritative director-approved storyboard blueprint

High-detail 3D illustration of the human liver, showing the Right Lobe, Left Lobe, and Gallbladder. Color palette: terracotta, deep brownish-red, and soft coral tones. Background: clean, soft beige or off-white. Lighting: soft studio style.
